name: write_tickets_tool
summary: Mutate the ticket queue: create new tickets or edit fields.
Use `create` with `assignee` to delegate work to another agent (their name) or scope it to a label.
Finishing a ticket is done with `write_result_tool`, not this tool.
constraints: `edit` defaults `key` to the agent's current ticket ‚Äî omit `key` to act on your own work.
`create` stamps `reporter` from the calling agent automatically; there is no `reporter` parameter.
anti_patterns: To look up, list, or search tickets ‚Äî use `read_tickets_tool`.
When the agent needs read AND write in the same flow ‚Äî use `manage_tickets_tool` instead of registering both.
To finish the agent's current ticket ‚Äî call `write_result_tool`.
To delegate work to another agent ‚Äî use `create` with `assignee` set to that agent's name; the loop's Path A picks it up next iteration.
cautions: IMPORTANT: this tool cannot transition status. Use `write_result_tool` to finish; `Failed` is reserved for ticket-system-driven outcomes (schema-retry trip, policy violations).
ALWAYS finish the current ticket with `write_result_tool` before the response ends. Returning text without a result leaves the ticket `InProgress` and the loop will re-pick it next iteration.
output: Plain text confirmation: `Created ticket KEY` or `Edited ticket KEY`.
On rejection: an error describing the failed action (missing field, unknown ticket).

key.description: Ticket key (e.g. `TICKET-3`). Required for `edit` (defaults to current ticket); ignored by `create`.

task.description: For `create` (required): the task body ‚Äî any JSON value (string, object, array, scalar). For `edit` (optional): replace the task body.

labels.description: For `create` or `edit` (optional): label scope. Determines which agents can pick up the ticket via Path B (label intersection).

assignee.description: For `create` (optional): a single label OR an agent name. If it matches a registered agent, the ticket is born `InProgress` with that assignee (Path A). Otherwise it is appended to `labels` (Path B).

schema.description: For `create` or `edit` (optional): a JSON Schema document. When set, the agent's final result (written via `write_result_tool`) must validate against it; failures count toward `max_schema_retries`.
